title: Identify the Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
text: To simplify a fraction, start by finding the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the numerator and the denominator. What is the GCD of $$144$$ and 192?

title: Calculate the GCD
text: Use the Euclidean algorithm to find the GCD of $$144$$ and $$192$$. Divide $$192$$ by $$144$$ and find the remainder. Then, divide $$144$$ by this remainder. Continue this process until the remainder is $$0$$. The last non-zero remainder is the GCD.

title: Divide by the GCD
text: Once you have the GCD, divide both the numerator and the denominator by this number to simplify the fraction.

title: Simplify the Fraction
text: Divide $$-144$$ and $$192$$ by their GCD, $$48$$. What is the simplified fraction?

title: Check Your Work
text: After simplifying, multiply the simplified numerator and denominator by the GCD to check if you get the original fraction. Does $$\frac{-3}{4}$$ multiplied by $$48$$ give you $$\frac{-144}{192}$$?
